task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the Company 'Horizon Aerial Services Pvt Ltd' and its corresponding Operator record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Django admin interface showing 'Horizon Aerial Services Pvt Ltd' in the Companies list or a success message for its creation. Also look for an Operator record linked to it showing 'Non-LUC' and 'SORA'.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of Person records for Arjun Mehta and Priya Sharma.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Django admin Persons list view or success messages showing 'Arjun Mehta' (arjun.mehta@horizonaerial.in) and 'Priya Sharma' (priya.sharma@horizonaerial.in).

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of Pilot records for both persons, linked to the Horizon Aerial operator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Django admin Pilots list view or success messages showing Pilot records for Arjun Mehta and Priya Sharma, explicitly linked to the 'Horizon Aerial' operator.

task_completion.description: Verify the registration of the two aircraft: HA-Scout-01 and HA-Scout-02.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Django admin Aircraft list view or success messages showing 'HA-Scout-01' and 'HA-Scout-02'. They should be marked as Active and linked to Horizon Aerial Services and the 'Horizon Surveyor X1' assembly.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Mumbai Harbor Survey' Flight Plan.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Django admin Flight Plans list view or a success message showing 'Mumbai Harbor Survey'. A detail view might show the GeoJSON polygon data.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'HA Fleet Certification Flight' Flight Operation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Django admin Flight Operations list view or a success message showing 'HA Fleet Certification Flight'. It must show links to aircraft 'HA-Scout-01', pilot 'Arjun Mehta', and the 'Mumbai Harbor Survey' plan.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with the Aerobridge Django admin interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser (like Chromium/Firefox) navigated to http://localhost:8000/admin/ rather than terminal curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Verify that the records were actually submitted to the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for Django's standard green success banners (e.g., 'The company was added successfully.') or the new items appearing as rows in the standard Django admin list views.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply type the expected output into a text editor or mock HTML file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface must be the genuine Aerobridge Django admin UI (typically a dark blue header with 'Django administration' and standard form layouts), not a generic text editor or visibly altered local HTML file.
